# Project Summary Report

**Report Date:** 2026-06-18 **Report Type:** Project Summary Discovery
**Generated By:** project-summary command (full rebuild mode)

## Investigation Scope

This report documents the investigative process used to rebuild the project
summary at `docs/PROJECT-SUMMARY.md`.

## Previous Summary Status

- **Last Updated:** 2026-05-29
- **Previous Status:** Early Development
- **Why a full rebuild:** the previous summary predates the spell era. Since
  2026-05-29 there have been **180 non-merge commits across 322 files**, and the
  whole spell-extraction completed — at the time of the old summary the spells
  did not yet live under `plugins/spellbook/skills/`; all six do now. Change
  volume alone (≥40 commits / ≥60 files) forced a full rebuild.

## Discovery Process

Foundation analysis (README, `package.json`, docs tree) was done directly. Four
read-only **Explore** subagents then ran in parallel for bounded scans:

1. **Spells scan** — each spell's `SKILL.md` + folder layout.
2. **Active projects scan** — `docs/projects/*` proposal headers.
3. **Recent activity scan** — 30 days of git + recent session notes.
4. **Craft/docs scan** — `grimoire/` + the `docs/` methodology subdirs.

Findings were synthesized by the orchestrator; a direct follow-up read resolved
the magpie discrepancy (below).

### Project Foundation Analysis

- **`package.json`:** name `spellbook`, version **1.7.0**, Bun module. Deps:
  React 19, react-dom, lucide-react, sharp. Dev: Biome, prettier, husky,
  lint-staged, tailwindcss 4, bun-plugin-tailwind. Scripts are format/lint only
  (no build/test at root — spells self-test via `bun test`).
- **Tooling:** Biome for `.ts/.tsx/.json` (error-on-warnings), prettier for
  `.md`, husky + lint-staged pre-commit, release-please for versioning.
- **Docs tree:** populated — `grimoire/`, `docs/projects/`, `docs/backlog/`,
  `docs/fragments/`, `docs/reports/`, the manifesto. Template-only —
  `docs/architecture/`, `docs/specifications/`, `docs/playbooks/`,
  `docs/lessons-learned/`, `docs/interaction-design/`, `docs/briefs/`,
  `docs/investigations/`.

### Documentation State

- **Architecture / Specifications:** templates only — no real content. The
  conceptual canon lives in `docs/PROJECT_MANIFESTO.md` (mirrored from Operator)
  and the operational canon in `grimoire/house-style.md`.
- **Active projects:** 9 folders (excluding `_archive`, `TEMPLATES`).
- **Craft system:** `grimoire/` is the live methodology home — house-style (~13
  rules), decay-ledger, trigger-registry, 6 fresh-agent reports, 11 scenarios.
  Authoring rituals `inscribe` + `ward` in `.claude/skills/`.

### Recent Activity Analysis (30 days)

- **imago:** layer system (containers/grouping/multi-select/transform),
  refs-as-assets (image unification), unified context library, surface UX.
- **glamour:** full React studio rebuild → shipped V1.0.
- **grapevine:** `announce` (cross-channel broadcast) + V1.7 (human as
  first-class participant, Alpine surface port).
- **bounty:** wave-2 (surface filters, card-aging, durability, `list` verb);
  SKILL finalized.
- **grimoire/docs:** co-presence captured as the shared spell shape;
  decay-ledger refinements; project archival.
- Frequent release-please cuts (v1.0 → v1.7).

### Code Structure Inspection

- **Conjurations (ship a daemon):** bounty (`server.ts`), glamour (`server.ts`),
  grapevine (`daemon.ts`), imago (`server.ts`).
- **Cantrips (no daemon):** digestify (one-shot review surface), magpie (Python
  `discover.py` + `extract.py`, one-shot asset extraction).
- **Surface tiers:** React + Tailwind studios (glamour, imago) vs Alpine-CDN
  templates (bounty, digestify, grapevine watch) vs no surface (magpie is a
  CLI).

## Key Findings

### What This Project Actually Is

Spellbook is a **Claude Code plugin marketplace of "spells"** — small, portable,
Bun-served browser surfaces an agent drives, with the agent as the runtime
underneath (no DB, no conventional server; auth/API at the MCP layer). It also
carries an unusually developed **craft system** (the grimoire) for authoring and
pruning those spells. It is both a product (six shipped spells) and a
methodology lab (how to grow agent surfaces well).

### Changes from Previous Summary

- **Scope:** went from an "under construction" platform shell to six shipped
  spells + a working grimoire. The toolbox→Spellbook extraction is complete.
- **New/matured systems:** glamour V1.0, imago (heavy post-V1 work), grapevine
  V1.7, bounty wave-2.
- **Conceptual:** the manifesto gained **co-presence** (§2, "the board, not the
  form") as the defining spell shape this very session.

### Current State Assessment

- **Maturity:** Active development, approaching a pre-release coherence pass.
- **Documentation:** strong where it counts (manifesto, grimoire, per-spell
  SKILLs); the formal `docs/architecture` + `docs/specifications` trees are
  unused (deliberately — the canon lives in manifesto + grimoire).
- **Activity:** high. **Direction:** clear (harden + cohere + rebrand spells
  toward a public release).

### Current Direction

- **In progress:** imago, glamour (image-style-spell).
- **Pre-release cohesion:** spellbook-coherence (align migrated spells),
  spellbook-rebrand (cute-occult aesthetic + renames).
- **Shipped recently:** grapevine-announce (merged), media-forge-cli-gaps
  (analysis report).
- **Backlog:** digestify-image-viewer, spell-architecture-maturity,
  grapevine-backlog.

## Insights & Observations

- The **grimoire is the differentiator** — a reinforcement-decay rule system
  (rules evaporate unless a fresh-agent finding or scenario re-walks them) that
  most projects don't have.
- **Two surface tiers** are an explicit design choice, not drift: React studios
  for rich canvases, Alpine-CDN for boards/reviews.
- The **manifesto is canonical in Operator**, mirrored to
  `docs/PROJECT_MANIFESTO.md`; re-sync only when the manifesto itself changes.

## Recommendations

- [x] **Reconcile magpie's listings (drift).** _Done 2026-06-18._ Its `SKILL.md`
      is an asset-extraction **cantrip** (Python CLI, no surface), but
      `grimoire/trigger-registry.md`, `plugins/spellbook/skills/README.md`, and
      the root `README.md` all described it as a **conjuration** / "image
      surface — drop, ask, orchestrate." Both kind and purpose corrected to
      "cantrip — extract individual assets from a composite image" across all
      three. (This same drift was first flagged in the 2026-05-29 report and had
      sat unfixed.)
- [ ] Consider whether the empty `docs/architecture` + `docs/specifications`
      template trees should be removed or kept as scaffolding (currently noise).
- [x] The root `README.md` is freshly written but inherited the magpie
      mis-description — _fixed 2026-06-18_ alongside the reconciliation above.
